title: F32t fix xHCI MSI programming order and eliminate input polling
description: Track F32t work to revalidate Linux xHCI MSI ground truth, apply Linux-order MSI programming, ensure MSI data matches the enabled GIC SPI, enable xHCI MSI delivery, remove timer-driven HID polling, and migrate BWM to wake-based input consumption.

title: F32s resume per-CPU resched Phase 3b
description: Resume from F32r commit a4380b7b. Re-apply the saved Phase 3b targeted wake diff one smaller piece at a time because the combined spawn/spawn_front/I/O wake batch failed wait_stress at sample 250 with a solid-blue render screenshot.

title: F32r per-CPU reschedule architecture
description: Audit and migrate Breenix reschedule signaling away from the global NEED_RESCHED flag toward targeted per-CPU resched semantics with Linux parity documentation and staged validation.
notes: Stopped after Phase 3b first attempted consumer batch failed validation. Passing commits: audit 2cb62b0d, design 3a2c4ce6, Phase 3a a4380b7b. Uncommitted Phase 3b batch was reverted; evidence and failed diff are in .factory-runs/f32r-percpu-resched/. Follow-up issue breenix-e43 filed.

title: F32o diagnose 800% CPU runaway
description: Diagnose current-main Breenix 800% host CPU runaway without fixes. Collect per-thread/per-CPU evidence, BWM loop state, idle-gate behavior, and write docs/planning/f32o-perf/diagnosis.md with root cause and recommended follow-up.
close_reason: Diagnosis complete: idle gate need_resched bypass identified; diagnosis document prepared.

title: F32p fix xHCI MSI/SPI input interrupts
description: Factory run to validate Linux xHCI MSI delivery on Parallels, audit Breenix against Linux, fix MSI/SPI delivery, remove timer-driven HID polling, migrate BWM input to wake-based delivery, and validate/merge.
notes: F32p stopped at Phase 3. Phase 1 Linux ground truth and Phase 2 Breenix-vs-Linux audit are committed on f32p-xhci-msi-interrupt. The attempted MSI fix (state.irq=early_irq + Linux-like PCI MSI ordering + early SPI enable) built cleanly but failed Parallels validation: clean /proc/xhci/trace samples stayed at event_count 0 / first_xfer_cc 255 and injected hotkeys did not reach BWM. Failed patch saved in .factory-runs/f32p-xhci-msi-interrupt-20260420-062743/phase3-xhci-msi.patch but not committed. Phase 4/5/6/7 not run; polling remains intentionally because MSI delivery was not proven. See exit.md in the run dir.

title: F32n store xHCI MSI IRQ in XhciState
description: Fix the F32n input regression by carrying the early xHCI MSI IRQ allocation into XhciState before XHCI_STATE is published, then validate clean builds, Parallels boot, wait_stress, and open a no-automerge PR for manual input smoke testing.

title: F32n event-driven input pipeline
description: Diagnose the HID-to-BWM input pipeline, document Linux event-driven input parity, fix the regression that prevents mouse/hotkeys from working, migrate BWM input consumption away from polling, validate, and merge.

title: F32l debug and fix ttwu_queue_wakelist regression
description: Investigate the F32k ttwu_queue_wakelist regression with trace evidence, re-audit Linux TTWU if needed, fix the identified scheduler/IPI issue, validate wait_stress and Parallels boots, then push/PR/merge if gates pass.

title: F32k ISR wake, op19 waitqueue, cursor restoration
description: Factory F32k: restore GUI cursor rendering, migrate ReadWindowInput op 19 to waitqueue, replace ISR deferred wake ring with Linux-parity ttwu_queue_wakelist IPI path, validate and merge.
notes:
Phase C implementation builds clean: removed ISR_WAKEUP_BUFFERS/isr_unblock_for_io, added per-CPU TTWU wake lists + SGI_CALL_FUNC handler, and removed F32j idle-gate wake-buffer scan. x86 qemu-uefi and aarch64 kernel builds pass cleanly.
Phase D failed. wait_stress rerun reported WAIT_STRESS_STALL sample=2 entered=6 returned=5 wakes=11621 waiters=0; CPU0 timer froze at tick 17 and subsequent AHCI Port 1 TIMEOUT failures prevented bwm/bsshd/bounce startup. Exit document: .factory-runs/f32k-ttwu-input-cursor-20260419-095521/exit.md

title: F32k Linux-style TTWU wake-list for ARM64 remote wakes
description: F32j implemented the idle sleep gate and GIC SGI admission fix, and wait_stress passed, but the required 5x Parallels 120s sweep stopped at run 3 because the exact CPU0 tick/window evidence gate was not satisfied. Implement F32i Option 2: a Linux-style target-CPU wake-list / function-call IPI path so remote task wake completion is drained on the target CPU, with tracing evidence and the same 5x Parallels gate.

title: F32j idle sleep gate and GIC SGI admission fix
description: Implement the F32j factory contract: Linux-style idle sleep gate for AArch64 CPU0 WFI lost-wake prevention, diagnose and fix GIC SGI admission where SGI0 pends in the redistributor but is not admitted by the CPU interface, validate wait_stress and 5x Parallels 120s, then PR/merge only if all gates pass.
notes: F32j code implemented and committed in two commits (idle sleep gate e7f20323, GIC SGI enable 946b2812). wait_stress passed, normal Parallels runs 1-2 passed, but run 3 failed the exact 5/5 evidence gate: strict render and frames passed, bsshd/bounce started, no AHCI timeout, but no clean [bounce] Window mode line and no cpu0 ticks= lines were present. Per contract, stopped without PR/merge; F32k follow-up filed for Linux-style TTWU wake-list.
close_reason: F32j implementation committed; wait_stress passed; 5x Parallels gate stopped at run 3, no PR/merge; follow-up breenix-k16 tracks Option 2

title: F32h AHCI ISR wake non-perturbing diagnosis
description: Collect non-serial evidence for the AHCI ISR-to-waiter wake stall, audit Linux IRQ-context completion semantics, and document proposed design options without implementing a fix.
close_reason: F32h diagnosis docs committed: evidence, Linux audit, and design proposal; no implementation changes.

title: F32e Linux prepare_to_wait waitqueue parity
description: Audit Breenix waitqueue blocking against Linux prepare_to_wait_event, implement the missing state-check-and-block behavior, validate wait_stress and Parallels gates, and merge only if all required gates pass.
notes: F32e stopped after validation failure. Audit commit e29d5abd and fix commit 3d0acbd3 are on branch f32e-linux-prepare-to-wait. wait_stress passed 60s with 0 stalls, but post-stress AHCI TIMEOUT occurred and normal Parallels run 1 stalled at /bin/bsshd with no bsshd success, no bounce, no render/FPS verdict. See docs/planning/f32e-linux-prepare-to-wait/exit.md.

title: F32c waitqueue deterministic reproducer and Linux parity fix
description: Factory F32c: build a deterministic waitqueue race reproducer, audit Breenix waitqueue ordering against Linux, apply the specific ordering fix, and validate without compositor timer fallbacks.
acceptance_criteria: wait_stress reproducer committed; ordering audit committed; Linux-parity fix committed; 60s wait_stress has 0 stalls; 5x120s Parallels sweep passes; no compositor BlockedOnTimer fallback remains
notes:
Phase 1 reproducer implemented and validated. Evidence: BREENIX_WAIT_STRESS=1 ./run.sh --parallels --test 90 produced WAIT_STRESS_STALL sample=3 entered=269 returned=268 wakes=17280 waiters=0 before BWM startup. Builds passed: aarch64 kernel, aarch64 userspace, qemu-uefi release.
Phase 4 failed. Phase 3 fix commit 1f6ae288 made the dedicated 60s wait_stress harness pass (final progress: entered=90983 returned=90982 wakes=3245248 waiters=0), but the same opt-in boot later hit AHCI timeouts with CPU0 tick_count stuck at 43299 and cpu0_breadcrumb=107. A clean normal './run.sh --parallels --test 120' then failed on run 1 by stopping at [spawn] path='/bin/bwm' with no bsshd, bounce, or FPS markers. Per F32c instructions, stopped without 5-run sweep, PR, or merge. Exit doc: docs/planning/f32c-waitqueue/exit.md.

title: F31 CPU0 WFI stall under SMP regression hunt
description: Investigate the post-F29 Parallels SMP regression where CPU0 stops taking timer ticks while PPI27 remains pending, bisect PR merge commits, diagnose CPU0+SMP interaction, and land a minimal fix or revert evidence PR.
notes: F31 partial/blocker: requested bisect completed. Evidence points to F28/PR #324 as regression boundary, not F29/PR #321. No production fix passed 120s Parallels gate; docs/planning/f31-cpu0-smp-stall/exit.md records failed attempts and raw artifact pointers.

title: F26 post-boot GUI lockup and bounce FPS regression
description: Investigate and fix the post-boot GUI lockup after visible rendering plus the bounce FPS regression observed after F25. Capture Parallels reproduction data, diagnose kernel-vs-userspace lockup, apply the minimal root-cause fix, remove or revisit the F25 bounce sleep, validate 120s GUI stability, and open/merge the PR.
close_reason: Fixed F26 GUI frame pacing and validated 120s Parallels run at 160 Hz with no fault markers

title: F25 fix post-bwm spawn hang
description: Investigate and fix the F25 hang where init spawn after /bin/bwm stops after printing the spawn path, including Phase 1 hello_raw narrowing, Phase 2 localization breadcrumbs or app diagnostics, Phase 3 fix, and Phase 4 Parallels/render validation.
close_reason: Fixed post-bwm spawn hang; validated bwm/telnetd/bsshd/bounce lifecycle and F23 render verdict.

title: F20a secondary CPU idle WFI fix
description: Investigate ARM64 secondary CPU idle hot-spinning under Parallels, add minimal WFI-based idle fix without changing timer/IRQ/GIC paths, and validate with five 45s Parallels runs plus host CPU sampling.
notes: F20a VERDICT FAIL on 2026-04-17. Multiple WFI/idle variants tested without touching timer_interrupt.rs, exception.rs, gic.rs, or syscall_entry.rs. Best single run passed boot with host_cpu_avg=128.5%, but required sweep run 1 failed: boot_script_completed=0, timer_tick_count=11, host_cpu_avg=795.7%, host_cpu_peak=800.0%. Kernel changes reverted; see docs/planning/f20a-idle-wfi/exit.md.

title: F20 Parallels display capture and GUI rendering fix
description: Factory F20: establish reliable Parallels guest screenshot capture, archive red baseline, then iterate on GUI rendering until captured display is distinguishable from solid red or four cycles are exhausted.
notes: Starting cycle 1 on branch fix/f20-c1-bwm-evidence. First step is fresh Parallels boot evidence and capture before patching.

title: F19 bsh runtime startup trace
description: Phase 2 for F19 after hello_raw confirmed post-exec userspace/fd/write/exit works. Instrument bsh startup from Rust runtime/main into shell initialization to find where bsh hangs silently before running /etc/init.js. Start from main after Phase 0 lands; restore init to /bin/bsh on the runtime-trace branch.
notes: F19 completed through Phase 3. Phase 0 hello_raw proved post-exec userspace/fd/write/exit. Phase 2 no-std padded probe plus exec breadcrumbs isolated the silent hang to overlapping early boot bsshd+bsh ELF reads stalling before file-content read completion. Phase 3 serializes early boot: init runs bsh first, bsh accepts PID 2 or 3 for init-script mode, bsshd starts after Boot script completed. Interactive ./run.sh --parallels reached [init] Boot script completed and [init] bsshd started.
close_reason: F19 completed: runtime trace isolated overlapping early exec reads; fix branch serializes boot shell before bsshd and interactive Parallels boot reaches [init] Boot script completed.

title: F19 hello_raw post-exec probe
description: Phase 0 for F19: add a minimal hello_raw userspace binary, temporarily exec it from init on ARM64 Parallels, capture serial evidence, and document whether the bug is kernel exec/fd inheritance or bsh runtime startup.
close_reason: Phase 0 complete: hello_raw printed after exec, exited 42, and init observed code 42; documented verdict directs F19 to Phase 2 bsh runtime trace.

title: F17 audit local IRQ-return scheduling after ISR wake
description: F16 removed the idle-CPU broadcast scan and replaced it with selected-target wake publication, but final sweep passed only 1/5. Selected-target SGI breadcrumbs were zero, so AHCI wakeups are local to the IRQ CPU. Audit whether per-CPU need_resched survives hardirq exit and whether AArch64 check_need_resched_and_switch runs for kernel threads blocked in the AHCI wait path.
acceptance_criteria: Cite the local IRQ return path, identify why local isr_unblock_for_io wakes do or do not schedule promptly, apply a minimal fix if found, and run the same 5x Parallels 60s sweep.

title: F16 idle-scan audit and fix
description: Audit isr_unblock_for_io idle CPU selection against Linux wake-up paths, apply the minimal scheduler fix, and run the required 5x Parallels sweep.
close_reason: F16 complete with FAIL verdict: audit and selected-target wake implementation committed; 5x Parallels sweep reached bsshd in all runs with corruption_markers=0 but only 1/5 had ahci_timeouts=0. Follow-up opened for local IRQ-return scheduling.

title: Capture equivalent Breenix CPU0 traces
description: Capture equivalent Breenix CPU0 traces for the same scenarios as the Linux reference set, using comparable instrumentation and preserving exact commands and artifacts.
notes: Reproducible Breenix Parallels CPU0 bundle captured at logs/breenix-parallels-cpu0/20260402-1454-live via scripts/parallels/collect-breenix-cpu0-traces.sh. Bundle shows EL0 entry, queue_empty stuck_tid warnings, PC_ALIGN at ELR=0xffff000040186801 from EL1, soft lockup, and continuing CPU0 timer progress afterward.

comments.author: Ryan Breen
comments.text: Current best hypothesis: blocked-in-syscall or kernel-mode ret-dispatch is resuming a thread with odd elr_el1 (0xffff000040186801, resolves inside ahci::handle_interrupt + 1). This is now the first concrete divergence candidate to falsify before any architectural fix.

title: Isolate first semantic divergence
description: Compare Linux and Breenix traces to isolate the first semantic divergence in CPU 0 timer, IPI, idle-exit, EL0 dispatch, or scheduling behavior. Hypotheses must be falsifiable and recorded with predicted observables.

title: Implement minimal architectural fix
description: Implement the minimal architectural fix for the first confirmed divergence. Remove workaround assumptions instead of layering new ones. The fix must be validated against the same canonical traces.

title: Capture canonical Linux CPU0 traces
description: Capture and preserve canonical Linux CPU0 traces on Parallels/HVF for timer, IPI, idle-exit, and scheduling paths. Treat these as the reference dataset for all later comparisons.
notes: Canonical Linux CPU0 trace bundle captured and verified in logs/linux-probe-cpu0/latest-smoke and logs/linux-probe-cpu0/20260402-073933. Linux on Parallels/HVF shows normal CPU0 arch_timer, IPI, idle-exit, and scheduling behavior.

title: ARM64 CPU0 interrupt-driven SMP investigation
description: Use Linux on Parallels/HVF as the reference model for CPU 0 interrupt-driven SMP semantics. No workaround-mode closures; every code change must be driven by an observed Linux-vs-Breenix divergence and closed only when CPU 0 sustains normal timer, IPI, idle-exit, EL0, and scheduling behavior without special routing.

title: Restore green F32s baseline validation harness
description: Before F32s wake-site conversion can proceed, prove the unchanged F32r base passes wait_stress with WAIT_STRESS_PASS and the quick Parallels boot gate. Current evidence in docs/planning/f32s/exit.md shows two baseline wait_stress failures before any code changes.

title: F32s staged per-CPU resched wake-site conversion
description: Convert scheduler wake sites one at a time to resched_cpu(target), validate each site with wait_stress and Parallels boot, then remove global NEED_RESCHED if all gates pass.
notes: Stopped in Phase 1 before code changes. Baseline validation is not green on F32r base: x86 boot-stages failed at TCP connect; two wait_stress Parallels baseline runs did not produce WAIT_STRESS_PASS. See docs/planning/f32s/exit.md.

title: F32q idle gate reads per-CPU need_resched
description: Implement the F32q surgical ARM64 idle gate fix so idle CPUs consult only per-CPU need_resched, then validate waitqueue, Parallels boot stability, and host CPU reduction.

title: F32m deterministic CPU0 tick-audit emission
description: Factory F32m: diagnose and fix nondeterministic [timer] cpu0 ticks audit emission so Parallels 5/5 gate observes the required evidence line without weakening parser gates.

title: F32i CPU0 WFI wake diagnosis and Linux validation
description: Diagnose why CPU0 remains idle after an IO wake enqueues init on CPU0, audit Linux WFI/IPI behavior, validate Linux behavior on the probe VM, and write design-only proposal documents. No implementation fixes.
close_reason: Completed F32i diagnosis, Linux audit, Linux probe validation, and design-only proposal docs.

title: F32f immediate waitqueue wake from task context
description: Audit Linux try_to_wake_up semantics against Breenix waitqueue wake deferral, implement immediate task-context waitqueue wake while preserving IRQ deferred wake path, validate wait_stress and Parallels boot criteria.
close_reason: F32f stopped per protocol: wait_stress passed after immediate wake, but Parallels run 1 failed at bounce spawn, so no PR/merge.

title: Follow up F32d FPS regression after CPU0 EL0 routing fix
description: F32d cleared the bwm-spawn AHCI timeout after the waitqueue fix, but validation stopped because the first normal 120s Parallels run missed the requested FPS >=160 gate (frame #15000 at cpu0 ticks=105000, estimated active fps=142.8). Use docs/planning/f32d-bwm-ahci-waitqueue/exit.md and artifacts under .factory-runs/f32d-bwm-ahci-waitqueue-20260418-142124/validation/boots/.

title: F32d fix BWM AHCI stall after waitqueue parity
description: Factory F32d: diagnose and fix the bwm-spawn AHCI stall exposed by F32c waitqueue Linux-parity ordering, preserving wait_stress correctness and validating 5x 120s Parallels boots.
notes:
F32d stopped before merge: wait_stress + post-stress boot passed, AHCI stall signature cleared, but first normal 120s Parallels run missed the requested FPS >=160 gate (frame #15000 at cpu0 ticks=105000, estimated active fps=142.8). Exit doc: docs/planning/f32d-bwm-ahci-waitqueue/exit.md
Validation stopped per prompt after run1 FPS failure; follow-up bead breenix-6f4 tracks the remaining FPS regression. Branch intentionally not merged.

title: F32b fix waitqueue compositor bounce spawn stall
description: Fix the F32 waitqueue compositor migration stall where bounce appears to block during frame pacing before bwm pumps frames. Confirm stall location with breadcrumbs, implement a non-polling fix, validate 5/5 Parallels runs, and merge.
notes: F32b stopped at Phase 3 gate. Phase 1 breadcrumbs showed timing-sensitive stall: uninstrumented diagnostic stopped at first op23 prepare with no op15 markers; adding an op23 prepared marker produced a passing 120s run with op15 wait/wake/exit. Attempted first-upload op15 skip built clean but Phase 3 run 1 failed before boot-script completion (boot=0 bsshd=0 bounce=0 tick=0 frame=1), so the source change was reverted and no PR/merge was attempted. Artifacts: .factory-runs/F32b-20260418-124014/.

title: Investigate F32 waitqueue Parallels stall before bounce spawn
description: F32 waitqueue branch passed clean builds but failed Phase 4 validation on the first rebuilt 120s Parallels run. Serial reached BWM and bsshd, then stopped at [spawn] path='/bin/bounce' with no SOFT LOCKUP/AHCI/DATA_ABORT/panic markers and only VirGL frames 0-1. See docs/planning/f32-waitqueue/exit.md and .factory-runs/f32-waitqueue-20260418-115626/rebuilt-run1.serial.log. Start with GDB around the bounce create_process_with_argv path and inspect waitqueue/scheduler lock interactions.
acceptance_criteria: Root cause identified and fixed without restoring polling fallback; aarch64 builds clean; F32 5-run 120s Parallels sweep passes all required lifecycle, CPU0 tick, no-AHCI, FPS, and render verdict gates.
notes: F32g Phase 1 committed: stall is parent init inside ARM64 Spawn at kernel/src/arch_impl/aarch64/syscall_entry.rs:1556 load_elf_from_ext2, before child creation. Wrote diagnosis.md and exit.md; no fix attempted.

title: F32 waitqueue primitive and compositor migration
description: Implement F32: design and add a Linux-style scheduler-integrated WaitQueue primitive, migrate compositor waits off the fallback timer, validate aarch64/Parallels behavior, and document exit results.
notes: Phase 4 stopped on 2026-04-18 after rebuilt Parallels run 1/5 failed. Serial reached [init] bsshd started (PID 4), then stopped at [spawn] path='/bin/bounce'; no SOFT LOCKUP/AHCI/DATA_ABORT/panic marker; render verdict passed but bounce lifecycle, CPU0 tick audit, and FPS gate were missing. Exit doc: docs/planning/f32-waitqueue/exit.md. Follow-up: breenix-bmv.

title: F29 re-enable Parallels ARM64 SMP
description: Factory F29: test removing the Parallels PSCI SMP gate, validate aarch64 boot/rendering, and either merge a passing SMP re-enable or document the unsafe fault signature honestly.
close_reason: F29 PASS: PR #321 merged; Parallels serial showed 8 CPUs online, boot script complete, bounce rendering at 160.87 Hz, no fault markers.

title: F28 eliminate 5ms compositor wake fallback
description: Factory F28 tracks instrumentation, reproduction, race fix, 120s validation, PR merge for eliminating compositor wake fallback hits.

title: F27 restore mouse cursor rendering
description: Investigate the mouse cursor rendering regression, localize HID/bwm/VirGL/display layer, restore cursor rendering without regressing F26 animation performance, validate with build and capture evidence, then open and merge PR.
close_reason: Merged PR #322 restoring cursor rendering on the aarch64 bwm direct-blit path with capture validation

title: F30 AHCI diagnostic scaffolding cleanup
description: Remove diagnostic scaffolding left by F8-F17/F19 after fixed AHCI IRQ admission, completion, and spawn hang bugs. Preserve behavior, validate each family removal with clean aarch64 build and 120s Parallels run.
close_reason: Completed in PR #323 (merged d6aebfa). Removed F30 diagnostic scaffolding with per-family validation and final 120s Parallels sweep.

title: F24 launch GUI apps on bwm desktop
description: Track F24 work to document ARM64 early userspace constraints, add a stricter render verdict, and incrementally launch GUI clients on the bwm desktop without regressing Parallels boot/capture.

title: F23 restore ARM64 GUI client spawning and op16 VirGL compositor
description: Follow-up from F22. bwm now takes over Parallels scanout and passes the rendered desktop bar capture using the ARM64 direct VirGL blit path, but full GUI client startup remains deferred. Restore script-driven ARM64 service launch for bterm/blog/bounce/bcheck, resolve the bsshd spawn/load stall observed after [spawn] path='/bin/bsshd', and fix the aarch64 op16 virgl_composite_windows_rect SUBMIT_3D timeout so bwm can return to the per-window VirGL compositor instead of direct blit.
notes: F23 PR #316 merged. This fixed the 75s Parallels render failure by adding strict verdicting and moving ARM64 /bin/bwm before /sbin/telnetd so the compositor renders before network services. Remaining broader follow-up from the original bead still exists: restore script-driven ARM64 GUI clients and op16 compositor work.

title: F22 bwm userspace GUI rendering on Parallels
description: Trace bwm lifecycle on single-CPU Parallels, fix the first missing checkpoint, validate non-blue rendered GUI capture, and merge the result.
close_reason: F22 completed in PR #315: Phase 1 findings committed, bwm desktop scanout takeover fixed on ARM64 Parallels, capture verdict GOOD with passes_rendered_desktop_bar=true. Follow-up full GUI client/op16 restoration tracked by breenix-6zk.

title: F21 debug Parallels VirGL scanout stays red after acknowledged flush
description: F20f exhausted four UI cycles with every prlctl capture still exactly solid red. Kernel logs successful VirGL SUBMIT_3D, cornflower-blue CLEAR, SET_SCANOUT, and RESOURCE_FLUSH before userspace, but Parallels display capture remains (255,0,0). Debug the virtio-gpu/VirGL scanout/update path below userland: scanout resource selection, flush/fence ordering, resource backing/format, and any missing host-visible update step after 3D rendering.
acceptance_criteria: A Parallels prlctl capture shows non-red rendered output from the kernel VirGL clear or compositor path; no polling; no prohibited interrupt/syscall hot-path edits.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #314: corrected F21 bisect, skipped Parallels SMP bring-up, restored VirGL cornflower baseline, and validated Parallels capture GOOD (100,149,237).

title: F20f Parallels screenshot capture and UI rendering fix
description: Implement F20f factory: first build reliable Parallels screenshot capture, then iterate on the solid-red UI rendering failure with captured verdicts and documentation.
close_reason: F20f factory complete: Phase 0 capture tooling landed and four UI cycles exhausted with honest red-capture exit. Follow-up filed as breenix-ml4 for F21 VirGL scanout debugging.

title: F20e audit side-effect bisect
description: Factory F20e: restore the F20d PER_CPU_IDLE_AUDIT diagnostic, bisect which operation re-enables CPU 0 timer delivery during idle, implement the minimal production fix, validate via sweep criteria, and document the exit.
notes: F20e partial/blocker documented in docs/planning/f20e-audit-bisect/exit.md. Baseline reproduced audit side effect 5/5; bisect isolated the load-bearing operation to the audit flag atomic RMW instruction (relaxed RMW sufficient). Production DSB-before-WFI candidate restored CPU0 timer trace and boot_script_completed=1 in the gate run, but failed host CPU gate (218.7% > 150%), so no PR/merge.

title: F20d: SGI probe for CPU0 post-timer-death interrupt delivery
description: F20c showed CPU0 tick_count freezes at 9 while CPUs 1-7 continue to ~32k, with PPI27 enabled/pending and CNTV_CTL=0x1. Add a one-shot non-timer kthread on a nonzero CPU that waits for CPU0 tick_count <= 10 while CPU1 tick_count > 1000, sends exactly one SGI to CPU0 via the existing GIC send path, and records whether CPU0 takes any interrupt or resumes timer ticks. Do not edit timer_interrupt.rs, exception.rs, syscall_entry.rs, or gic.rs without explicit approval.
acceptance_criteria: A --test 45 boot captures before/after SGI CPU0 tick_count, CPU0_BREADCRUMB_ID, CPU0_LAST_TIMER_ELR, CNTV_CTL, and GICR_ISPENDR0; phase docs state whether SGI wakes CPU0 and whether timer ticks resume; no polling fallback.

title: F20c CPU0 WFI wake after valid timer/GIC state
description: F20b audit showed CPU0 enters scheduler-idle WFI with CNTV_CTL_EL0=0x1, near-future CVAL, PPI27 enabled in GICR_ISENABLER0, ICC_PMR_EL1=0xf0, ICC_IGRPEN1_EL1=1, DAIF.I/F clear, and ICC_RPR_EL1=0xff, but CPU0 does not emit post_wfi before boot completion. Continue from docs/planning/f20b-per-cpu-timer-wake/exit.md and docs/planning/ARM64_CPU0_SMP_INVESTIGATION.md. Avoid prohibited files and do not add polling fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Identify CPU0-specific WFI wake/delivery blocker after valid pre-WFI state; commit a minimal fix; validate ./run.sh --parallels --test 45 5/5 with host_cpu_avg <150%, boot_script_completed=1, timer_tick_count >=24.
notes: F20c diagnostic completed on branch fix/f20c-cpu0-idle. Phase 1 commit b52a154b added end-of-boot per-CPU tick audit; docs commit 61f96f06 records FAIL/no-fix verdict. H2 confirmed for the observable main --test 45 path: CPU0 tick_count stayed at 9 while CPUs 1-7 reached ~32k, with PPI27 enabled/pending and CNTV_CTL=0x1. No fix committed; follow-up SGI probe filed as breenix-cb7.

title: F20b per-CPU timer wake at WFI idle
description: Investigate and fix AArch64 per-CPU virtual timer interrupt delivery when CPUs enter WFI scheduler idle. Capture one-shot idle audit state, apply minimal interrupt delivery fix, validate five-run sweep, and document results.
close_reason: F20b diagnostic audit completed and documented; candidate masks disproved for CPU0, no safe fix found; follow-up filed as breenix-9f1.

title: Harden ARM64 early-boot concurrent ext2/AHCI exec reads
description: F19 fixed the boot-visible bsh silent hang by serializing early boot execs, but diagnostics showed overlapping large ELF reads from /bin/bsshd and the boot shell can stall in the ext2/AHCI file-content read path. Investigate and fix the underlying concurrent-read robustness without relying on init ordering.

title: F17 local IRQ-return wake-buffer audit
description: Factory F17: instrument ARM64 local wake ordering after F16 selected-target wake publication, apply a minimal need_resched/wake-buffer drain fix if indicated, run the 5x Parallels sweep, and document verdict.
close_reason: F17 completed: breadcrumbs + 5-run sweep documented; verdict FAIL/Other, H1 and H2 falsified; branch pushed as diagnostic-fix/f17-local-wake.

title: F15: investigate remaining ARM64 AHCI timeout corridor after GICR discovery
description: F14 Linux-style GICR redistributor discovery found all 8 RD frames and fixed non-CPU0 GICR_STATE reads, but the 5x Parallels sweep still failed: runs 2 and 3 each had two AHCI port timeouts with corruption_markers=0. Next direction from docs/planning/ARM64_CPU0_SMP_INVESTIGATION.md: target the PMR divergence (Breenix ICC_PMR_EL1=0xf8 vs Linux 0xf0) or inspect the idle-scan/send_sgi caller handoff where UNBLOCK_BEFORE_SEND_SGI can appear without a matching newest UNBLOCK_AFTER_SEND_SGI.
acceptance_criteria: Run a follow-up probe sweep under Parallels; document whether PMR or idle-scan/send_sgi changes eliminate AHCI timeouts without corruption; preserve prohibited interrupt/syscall path constraints.

title: F14 GICR discovery sweep
description: Implement Linux-style GICR redistributor discovery and run the F14 validation sweep.

title: F12 SGI Linux parity probe
description: Run F12 SGI Linux parity probe: compare Linux GICv3 SGI emission, patch Breenix send_sgi ordering and SRE audit, run 5x Parallels sweep, and document verdict.
close_reason: F12 completed with FAIL verdict: Linux SGI ordering parity implemented and documented; 5x sweep still shows AHCI timeouts in 2/5 runs; F13 should pivot to per-CPU ICC/GICR redistributor audit.

title: F11 send_sgi and wakebuf breadcrumb sweep
description: Add fine-grained AHCI ring breadcrumbs inside aarch64 gic::send_sgi and around IsrWakeupBuffer::push, run the required five-sample Parallels validation sweep, and document the F12 recommendation.
close_reason: F11 send_sgi/wakebuf breadcrumb sweep completed; branch diagnostic/f11-send-sgi-boundary has code and investigation commits.

title: F6 GIC stuck-state diagnostic capture
description: Add observational-only ARM64 GIC stuck-state dumps for SPI34 on AHCI timeout and fatal postmortem, run the Parallels sweep, and document the results for F7 probe selection.
close_reason: Closed

title: Remove workarounds and add regression verification
description: Remove CPU 0 workaround code once the real fix is proven, then add repeatable verification so the project cannot regress back into workaround mode unnoticed.

title: Factory F1: extract Linux v6.8 GIC-v3 IRQ blueprint
description: Produce a cite-backed prose blueprint of Linux ARM64 v6.8 GIC-v3 IRQ handling from EL1 IRQ entry through EOIR and return, using local or probe-host source and the existing linux-probe evidence. Deliver the blueprint, scratchpad, and exit documentation in a repo-local factory run directory without modifying the Breenix kernel tree.
close_reason: Closed
